title: My Mom the Doctor
story_html: My mom is a doctor. She is hardworking and patient. She helps sick people every day. At the zoo, I see a giraffe. It eats leaves from a tall tree. Zebras walk together in a herd. They are not the same as horses. A tiger has sharp claws. It uses them to hunt for food. Near the window, I eat my lunch. I have warm noodles today. My sister wants to share my food with me.

giraffe.def: A tall animal with a very long neck.

claws.def: The sharp, curved nails on an animal's feet.

herd.def: A large group of animals that live and move together.

window.def: An opening in a wall, usually with glass.

share.def: To use or have something together with others.

noodles.def: Long, thin strips of food made from flour.

patient.def: Able to wait calmly without getting upset.

hardworking.def: Working with a lot of effort.

doctor.def: A person whose job is to treat sick people.

questions.qtext: "Zebras walk together in a herd." What does herd mean?
questions.explain: A herd is a large group of animals that live and move together.
Claws are the sharp, curved nails on an animal's feet.
The story says, "My mom is a doctor."

questions.qtext: What animal does the child see eating leaves at the zoo?
questions.explain: The story says the giraffe eats leaves from a tall tree.

questions.qtext: What food does the child have for lunch?
questions.explain: The story says, "I have warm noodles today."
